stories.headline: Venezuela capital hit by double earthquake as experts warn of high death toll
stories.summary: Delcy Rodríguez declares state of emergency after buildings collapse across Caracas, with tremors felt in ColombiaVenezuela earthquake – latest updatesVenezuela’s interim leader, Delcy Rodríguez, has declared a state of emergency after the country was struck by two powerful earthquakes that collapsed dozens of buildings and killed at least 32 people, with experts warning the death toll could rise significantly.Rodríguez said hundreds were injured

stories.headline: Europe Today: Ukraine seeks defence, energy deals in Poland as Macron meets Meloni in France
stories.summary: The Ukraine Recovery Conference 2026 kicks off today in Gdańsk, with Kyiv aiming to secure deals on defence capabilities, energy and economic cooperation. Meanwhile, France’s President Emmanuel Macron welcomes Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni to the French Riviera today for a major summit.

stories.headline: Iraq Warns It Might Quit OPEC If It Doesn’t Get Quota Hike
stories.summary: Iraq said it might one day consider leaving OPEC unless the group accommodates it with a higher oil production quota, just a few months after the United Arab Emirates quit the group so that it could pump more.

stories.headline: PBOC Plans Overnight Reverse Repo in Next Stage of Policy Shift
stories.summary: China’s central bank is introducing an overnight tenor into its open-market operations, a key step toward reshaping how it steers short-term borrowing costs.

stories.summary: In a late-night vote aimed at mollifying the president, Senate Republicans rejected a resolution directing him to end the war against Iran, a day after a bipartisan rebuke.

stories.headline: Saudis Set to Restart Ras Tanura Oil Exports as Gulf Flows Rise
stories.summary: Top oil exporter Saudi Arabia appears to be about to restart loading crude at the Ras Tanura terminal inside the Persian Gulf, more than a week after a US-Iran peace deal was struck to free up the region’s petroleum flows.

stories.headline: Chinese Crushers Seek Australian Canola on Trade Pact Hopes
stories.summary: Chinese oilseed crushers have made inquiries for fresh cargoes of Australian canola in anticipation of a trade pact that could end a years-long freeze on supplies.

stories.headline: Dubai Holding Weighs Buying Data Center Stake
stories.summary: Dubai Holding is exploring an investment in private equity-backed data center builder Hscale, Alex Dooler reports.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Hungary Yields Approach UK Gilts as Pro-EU Turn Fuels Rally
stories.summary: Hungary’s bonds have rallied so much that yields are approaching those in the UK, the latest sign that investors are backing the new government’s plans to fix the economy and join the euro region.

stories.headline: SK Hynix ADR Seen Driving 30% Upside as Micron Gap Narrows
stories.summary: SK Hynix Inc.’s push for a US listing is spurring talk of another prize for shareholders: a potentially higher market value that may narrow its gap with US rival Micron Technology Inc.

stories.summary: Bitcoin is facing a massive options expiry that risks putting more pressure on a market already struggling with fading institutional demand and macroeconomic headwinds.

stories.headline: Engines and Elevators Lift German Deals Past $120 Billion
stories.summary: Germany’s dealmakers are seeing some of their long-wished-for transactions come to fruition, driving the country to one of its busiest years for mergers and acquisitions in decades.

stories.headline: EasyJet Shows Willingness to Talk After Next Castlelake Bid

stories.headline: Costly IVF add-ons fail to show benefits, new study finds
stories.summary: A range of extra treatments marketed by fertility clinics to improve the chances of success during in vitro fertilisation (IVF) lacks convincing evidence that they work, according to a new study published in the scientific journal The Lancet.

stories.headline: The 4 GOP senators who broke ranks and voted to block Trump from resuming Iran war
stories.summary: The president seethed at Susan Collins, Bill Cassidy, Lisa Murkowski and Rand Paul after the war powers resolution passed, saying: “I will get it done, one way or the other.”

stories.headline: White House seeks $88 billion from Congress for Iran war

stories.headline: UN says it will evacuate sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz, as Rubio warns against tolls

stories.headline: Iran says no new commitments on nuclear sites after Vance says inspectors to be invited back
